info.name: Book Service MVP - API & Integration Testing
info.description:
API & integration smoke collection for Book Service MVP.

**Run order (local mutating):** Folder 1 upstream → Folder 2 synthetic webhook → Folder 1 downstream.

**Variable chaining:** `auditLeadId` ← audit; checkout retrieves real `orderId` + metadata from Stripe session; Folder 2 webhook uses that metadata; `userId`/`userToken` ← create-account; `fixJobId` = `orderId`.

**Safety:** `runMutatingSmoke=false` skips writes (production default). Folder 2 requires `allowSyntheticWebhook=true` AND `baseUrl` localhost — hard-blocked on production hosts. Stripe session retrieve requires `sk_test_` key only.

item.name: 1 — Health / Smoke API (upstream)
item.description: Read-only + audit + checkout. Run Folder 2 next before downstream requests.

item.name: 4 — Performance / Load Spot Checks (§4.4)
item.description: Lightweight sanity only — not load testing. Audit timing is asserted in Folder 1 upstream. This folder: short bursts on checkout (validation 400s, fast) and analytics (200s) plus immediate recovery requests. Different from Folder 10 rate-limit burst (429 hunting).

item.name: Burst stability — POST /api/book-service/checkout (15× validation, no hang)

exec: stats.push({ code: res && res.code, ms: res && res.responseTime, err: !!err });

item.name: 5 — Auth & Account Recovery
item.description: Auth boundary checks (run before password reset — reset revokes existing ID tokens) + password reset flow. Chains testEmail/userToken/userId from Folders 1–2. Reset tokens are app-generated Firestore passwordResets/{token}, not Firebase Auth OOB — devResetToken returned when FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST is set. Unknown-email test asserts identical generic success response (anti-enumeration).

item.name: PATCH protected route — no auth header (401)
GET admin route — valid non-admin token (403)

item.name: 7 — Admin Fix Jobs (full pipeline)
item.description: Admin fix-job pipeline per test plan §3.5. Seeds from Folders 1–2 chained fixSession (orderId=fixJobId, userId, adminToken). Stage transitions use PATCH /stage (required by route code). QA is PATCH (not POST). Report path is POST /report/generate. Verifies Firestore state via GET /api/admin/fix-jobs/:id?uid= after each stage change.

item.name: 9 — Analytics / Notifications / External Side Effects
item.description: Analytics Firestore writes + validation-only checks on notification routes. EXCLUDED from automated happy-path calls (would hit real Loops/Notion): POST /api/notifications/new-user (valid body), POST /api/notifications/dashboard-ready (valid email), POST /api/notifications/account-created (valid email), GET /api/cron/weekly-sales-digest. GET /api/test-sentry-error posts to real Sentry — included with explicit flag; delivery needs manual verification.

item.name: 10 — Negative / Security / Abuse Cases
item.description: Adversarial cross-section: public (audit, checkout, order-status), authenticated (confirm-details, create-account), admin (fix-jobs). Every test asserts status codes AND response bodies contain no stack traces, file paths, or node_modules leaks. Rate-limit burst may not 429 locally when Upstash is unreachable (fail-open) — documented as FINDING, not a pass on broken behavior.

item.name: Setup — capture token before revoke (Folder 10 auth abuse)

exec: pm.test('No stack trace or internal paths in body', () => {
